summary: President Trump announced the cancellation of planned U.S. strikes against Iran, citing senior Iranian approval of a deal (Source: @almogboker78/17452). Israeli forces reported advancing in southern Lebanon, seizing underground tunnels and Hezbollah militants (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73445). Iran’s IRGC warned of a severe response to any future U.S. attacks and confirmed a destroyed aircraft at Bandar Abbas Airport after recent strikes (Source: @shin_persian/6958, @shin_persian/6957).
key_points: Trump announced cancellation of U.S. strikes on Iran after senior Iranian leadership approved a deal, keeping the naval blockade in place (Source: @almogboker78/17452).
Israeli ground forces advanced in southern Lebanon, capturing several underground tunnels and Hezbollah equipment (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73445).
IRGC threatened a harsher response to any renewed U.S. attack, calling it a potential regional destabilizer (Source: @shin_persian/6958).
Satellite imagery showed an aircraft destroyed at Bandar Abbas Airport following the latest U.S. strikes (Source: @shin_persian/6957).
Hezbollah militants were killed in a tunnel near Bofar after detection by an Israeli drone and subsequent air strike (Source: @inon_yttach/8901).
Iranian Revolutionary Guard claimed to have targeted five military bases in Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain since last night (Source: @kianmeli1/87925).
Iranian news agency Fars reported that Iran has not yet provided a final response to U.S. negotiation proposals (Source: @asafroz/50182).

sections.executive_summary: Trump cancelled planned U.S. strikes on Iran after senior Iranian approval, keeping the naval blockade active. Israeli forces advanced in southern Lebanon, seizing tunnels and Hezbollah fighters. Iran warned of a severe retaliation to any future U.S. attacks and confirmed damage to a Bandar Abbas aircraft.

points: President Trump announced the cancellation of U.S. strikes on Iran, stating that senior Iranian leadership approved the agreement and the naval blockade will remain until the deal is finalized (Source: @almogboker78/17452).
IRGC headquarters warned that any renewed U.S. attack on Iran would provoke a response more severe than any previous engagement, threatening regional destabilisation (Source: @shin_persian/6958).
Satellite imagery released by OSINT sources showed an aircraft destroyed at Bandar Abbas Airport after the latest U.S. strikes, with coordinates 27.20909, 56.36896 (Source: @shin_persian/6957).
Iranian news agency Fars reported that Iran has not yet submitted a final response to the U.S. negotiation proposals, indicating ongoing deliberations (Source: @asafroz/50182).
Fars also noted that Iran has not provided a final document in the talks with the United States, contradicting claims of an agreement (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/369113).
Iranian Revolutionary Guard claimed to have targeted five military bases across Jordan, Kuwait, and Bahrain since the previous night, naming specific air bases (Source: @kianmeli1/87925).
Iranian officials discussed potential travel to Pakistan by the foreign minister, hinting at diplomatic outreach (Source: @SharghDaily/165332).

Israeli ground forces reported advancing in southern Lebanon, capturing several underground tunnels and Hezbollah terrorist equipment, indicating expanding operational success (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73445).
IDF tank unit conducted its first maneuver in Gaza and later operated in Lebanon, with the commander now leading an armor school (Source: @idf_telegram/24294).
Israeli forces eliminated Hezbollah militants hiding in Bofar tunnels after detection by a Yael drone, followed by an air strike that destroyed the fleeing militants (Source: @inon_yttach/8901).
A report highlighted Israeli tank positions along the Saluki River confronting Hezbollah, emphasizing ongoing frontline engagements (Source: @idf_telegram/24292).
An Israeli source warned of possible Iranian retaliation to Israeli actions, linking regional escalation to broader US‑Iran dynamics (Source: @pkpoi/142670).
Israeli officials discussed readiness for both defense and offense amid potential Iranian responses, stressing public updates (Source: @pkpoi/142670).
Hezbollah’s underground network remains a target, with Israeli drones and air assets actively seeking and destroying tunnel complexes (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73445).
IDF tank unit performed its first maneuver in Gaza, marking a new operational phase in the enclave (Source: @idf_telegram/24294).
No specific civilian casualty figures or new ground operations were reported beyond the tank maneuver (Source: @idf_telegram/24294).
Israeli media noted ongoing military preparedness but provided no new incidents in the West Bank (Source: @pkpoi/142670).
No additional developments were identified in the provided messages for Gaza or the West Bank.
Israeli ground forces advanced in southern Lebanon, seizing multiple underground tunnels and Hezbollah equipment, indicating a widening operational footprint (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73445).
Hezbollah militants were killed in a Bofar tunnel after Israeli drone detection and subsequent air strike (Source: @inon_yttach/8901).
Reports suggested possible movement of heavy military equipment toward the Lebanese border by forces linked to Ahmad al-Shar'aa (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73448).
Israeli tanks were positioned along the Saluki River confronting Hezbollah positions, highlighting ongoing frontline tension (Source: @idf_telegram/24292).
No civilian casualty figures were provided in the messages concerning Lebanon.
No direct operational reports from Syria or Iraq were present in the supplied messages.
Iranian statements warned of broader regional conflict if the U.S. resumes attacks, implicitly referencing Syria and Iraq as potential theatres (Source: @shin_persian/6958).
Iranian Revolutionary Guard claimed targeting bases in neighboring Jordan, Kuwait, and Bahrain, suggesting a regional reach that could affect Syria/Iraq (Source: @kianmeli1/87925).
Trump’s cancellation statement listed Gulf states (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain) among parties approving the Iran deal (Source: @almogboker78/17452).
Iranian Revolutionary Guard targeted bases in Kuwait and Bahrain, indicating direct action against Gulf military assets (Source: @kianmeli1/87925).
Fars reported that Iran has not yet given a final response to U.S. proposals, affecting Gulf diplomatic calculations (Source: @asafroz/50182).
Iranian officials discussed reopening the Strait of Hormuz during a 60‑day cease‑fire, a critical Gulf shipping lane (Source: @SharghDaily/165329).
No further Gulf‑state specific military movements were detailed in the messages.

key_developments: President Trump announced the cancellation of planned U.S. strikes against Iran after senior Iranian leadership approved a deal, keeping the naval blockade in effect (Source: @almogboker78/17452).
Israeli ground forces advanced in southern Lebanon, capturing several underground tunnels and Hezbollah equipment, expanding their operational reach (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73445).
Hezbollah militants were killed in a Bofar tunnel after detection by an Israeli drone and subsequent air strike, demonstrating effective Israeli ISR and strike capability (Source: @inon_yttach/8901).
Iran’s IRGC warned that any renewed U.S. attack would trigger a response more severe than any previous engagement, threatening regional destabilisation (Source: @shin_persian/6958).
Satellite imagery confirmed an aircraft destroyed at Bandar Abbas Airport following the latest U.S. strikes, with precise coordinates provided (Source: @shin_persian/6957).
Iranian Revolutionary Guard claimed to have targeted five military bases in Jordan, Kuwait, and Bahrain since the previous night, naming specific air bases (Source: @kianmeli1/87925).
Iranian news agency Fars reported that Iran has not yet provided a final response to U.S. negotiation proposals, indicating ongoing deliberations (Source: @asafroz/50182).
sections.threat_assessment: The cancellation of U.S. strikes on Iran reduces immediate direct conflict but leaves a naval blockade and diplomatic uncertainty, maintaining pressure on Tehran. Israeli advances in southern Lebanon and successful strikes on Hezbollah tunnels increase the risk of escalation with Lebanese militias. Iran’s explicit threats of a harsher response to any future U.S. action, combined with reported attacks on Gulf and regional bases, suggest a willingness to expand the conflict beyond its current borders.
sections.regional_response: Gulf states, listed among parties approving the Iran deal, are likely to monitor the naval blockade and any shifts in U.S. policy closely. Israel’s ground operations in Lebanon may prompt Hezbollah retaliation, potentially drawing Lebanese civilian areas into conflict. Iran’s statements and base attacks indicate a strategy of signaling capability to deter further U.S. or allied actions while keeping diplomatic channels open.
sections.intelligence_notes: Future monitoring should focus on any movement of Iranian Revolutionary Guard assets toward Jordan, Kuwait, and Bahrain, as well as additional Israeli tunnel‑clearing operations in Lebanon. Satellite surveillance of Bandar Abbas and surrounding airfields will verify the extent of aircraft losses. Open‑source tracking of diplomatic talks, especially involving Qatar and the UAE, will clarify whether the announced agreement progresses toward signing.
